# Learn to Upholster — The Working Upholsterer's Bible

> The complete text of The Working Upholsterer's Bible by Shaun Greenwood (master upholsterer, AMUSF accredited, Greenwood Upholstery, Hebden Bridge), published free online. Traditional and modern upholstery: materials, techniques, full step-by-step projects, workshop stories, and free calculators.

## Chapters and pages

- [A Brief, Opinionated History of Upholstery](https://www.learntoupholster.com/a-brief-opinionated-history-of-upholstery): A brief, opinionated history of upholstery — from mediaeval cushions and Tudor state beds to Pratt’s coil spring, the staple gun, and today’s craft revival.
- [A–Z glossary of upholstery terms](https://www.learntoupholster.com/a-z-glossary): An A–Z glossary of around 150 upholstery terms — from antimacassar to zigzag spring — with plain-English definitions, cross-referenced to the relevant chapters.
- [About](https://www.learntoupholster.com/about): Learn to Upholster is a free upholstery reference and the companion site to the book The Working Upholsterer’s Bible, written by master upholsterer Shaun Greenwood.
- [Buttoning & Tufting — deep buttoning, the diamond grid & float buttoning](https://www.learntoupholster.com/buttoning-and-tufting): Deep buttoning and tufting explained: the symmetric diamond grid, how a button is pulled through the pad to a backing patch, the diagonal pleats, and the.
- [Calico, Wadding & Top Cover — fitting the cover, corner pleats & panel order](https://www.learntoupholster.com/calico-wadding-and-top-cover): Fitting an upholstery cover properly: the calico under-cover as a dress rehearsal, the wadding that smooths it, cutting and fitting the show fabric, the.
- [Chesterfield Sofa — Deep-Buttoned Leather Restoration](https://www.learntoupholster.com/chesterfield-sofa): The Chesterfield restoration end to end: the form, materials, button counts by size, deep buttoning in leather, dampening pleats, brass-nail trim at 25 mm.
- [Choosing the Right Fabric — a beginner's guide](https://www.learntoupholster.com/choosing-the-right-fabric): How to choose upholstery fabric: reading a fabric label, the eight fibre families, Martindale and Wyzenbeek rub ratings and which band you need, pattern.
- [Contact](https://www.learntoupholster.com/contact): Get in touch with Learn to Upholster — questions about the site, the book or the tools. Email shaun@greenwoodupholstery.com.
- [Contents — Learn to Upholster](https://www.learntoupholster.com/contents): The full contents of The Working Upholsterer's Bible — foundations, techniques, complete projects, the working workshop, and reference. Free to read.
- [Cookie Policy](https://www.learntoupholster.com/cookie-policy): The cookies and similar technologies Learn to Upholster uses, including functional storage and Google AdSense advertising cookies, how consent is collected.
- [Customers & the workshop year — running an upholstery business](https://www.learntoupholster.com/customers-and-the-workshop-year): The other half of the upholstery business: handling the first customer conversation, managing expectations, the seasonal rhythm of the workshop year, and.
- [Deep-Buttoning & Diamond Calculator](https://www.learntoupholster.com/deep-buttoning-calculator): A free deep-buttoning calculator for upholsterers: work out the diamond button layout and count, the base and fabric marking grids, the fabric fullness.
- [Disclaimer](https://www.learntoupholster.com/disclaimer): Important information about relying on Learn to Upholster: general guidance only, safety and professional judgement, fire regulations, accuracy, and affiliate disclosure.
- [Drop-In Dining Seat — A Complete Beginner’s Project](https://www.learntoupholster.com/drop-in-dining-seat): How to reupholster a drop-in dining chair seat, step by step: stripping, webbing, stuffing, the top cover and the bottoming cloth.
- [Fabric Yardage for Upholstery — Metric & Imperial Calculator](https://www.learntoupholster.com/fabric-yardage): How much fabric do you need to reupholster a chair or sofa? A free upholstery fabric calculator in metric and imperial, a rule-of-thumb yardage chart, and a.
- [Find an Upholsterer Near You](https://www.learntoupholster.com/find-an-upholsterer): How to find a good upholsterer near you: search the vetted AMUSF member directory in the UK, find local upholsterers on the map anywhere, and the questions.
- [UK Fire Regulations Checker for Upholstery](https://www.learntoupholster.com/fire-safety-checker): What do the UK furniture fire regulations require of your upholstery job? Answer four questions and get plain-English guidance on covers, fillings, interliners and records — plus a printable compliance record.
- [Foam Construction — the two-foam cushion, cutting, bonding & the Dacron wrap](https://www.learntoupholster.com/foam-construction): Modern foam upholstery, step by step: the two-foam principle (HD base plus soft top), choosing foam by density, cutting with a serrated knife, spray-bonding.
- [Foam & Cushion Spec — Density by Project](https://www.learntoupholster.com/foam-cushion-calculator): A free foam selector for upholstery: pick the application and get the right foam type, density, firmness, depth, Dacron wrap and fire grade (CMHR / Crib 5).
- [Frame Repair & Joint Reinforcement — fixing chair frames](https://www.learntoupholster.com/frame-repair-and-joint-reinforcement): The frame repairs every upholsterer should handle: re-gluing loose mortise-and-tenon joints with hide glue, splicing a worn-out tacking rail, and treating.
- [Headboard — Upholstered Headboard Project](https://www.learntoupholster.com/headboard): How to make or re-cover an upholstered headboard: UK sizes for every bed, the ply-foam-Dacron-fabric build, float buttoning, and strut vs wall (French.
- [Historical style guide — identifying British chair & sofa forms](https://www.learntoupholster.com/historical-style-guide): A quick-lookup reference to the named chair and sofa forms most common in British upholstery — Georgian, Victorian and Howard wing-backs, Chesterfield.
- [Learn to Upholster — the complete craft, traditional and modern](https://www.learntoupholster.com/): A free, in-depth upholstery reference from a master upholsterer with thirty years at the bench.
- [Upholstery knots & stitches — illustrated reference](https://www.learntoupholster.com/knots-and-stitches): An illustrated reference to the knots and stitches of traditional upholstery — bridle, blind and top stitches; the clove hitch, slip knot and square (reef).
- [Leather Hide Calculator — Square Feet & Hides](https://www.learntoupholster.com/leather-hide-calculator): How much leather do you need to upholster a chair or sofa? A free leather hide calculator: pick the piece or convert a fabric estimate to square feet, allow.
- [Loose Covers — measuring, the calico mock-up, panels & seams](https://www.learntoupholster.com/loose-covers): Making a loose cover (slipcover): measuring the chair, cutting a calico mock-up, the main panels, sewing on a walking-foot machine, French versus straight.
- [Materials — A Field Guide](https://www.learntoupholster.com/materials-a-field-guide): A field guide to every material in an upholstered chair: frame timbers, jute and rubber webbing, hessian, scrim and calico, horsehair, coir and foam, twines.
- [Upholstery materials reference charts — specs & uses](https://www.learntoupholster.com/materials-reference-charts): Reference charts for every upholstery material — webbing, hessian and scrim, stuffings, springs, twines and cord, tacks, staples and nails — with.
- [Modern Sofa Re-Cover — Fast, Fixed-Price, Foam & Staple](https://www.learntoupholster.com/modern-sofa-recover): How to re-cover a modern factory sofa: the foam-and-staple construction, using the old cover as cutting templates, zipped cushion covers, and how to price the job.
- [Our Work — Upholstery Gallery](https://www.learntoupholster.com/our-work): Real pieces reupholstered by hand at Greenwood Upholstery in West Yorkshire — wing chairs, Victorian show-frames, mid-century settees, footstools and.
- [Pricing & quoting for an upholstery workshop](https://www.learntoupholster.com/pricing-and-quoting): How to price upholstery work and build a quote that survives: setting an hourly rate from an income target, the materials / labour / contingency breakdown.
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.learntoupholster.com/privacy-policy): How Learn to Upholster collects and uses information, including cookies, Google AdSense advertising, analytics and affiliate links, and your data-protection rights.
- [Reupholstery Cost & Time Estimator](https://www.learntoupholster.com/reupholstery-cost-calculator): A free reupholstery cost calculator: pick your piece, build type and fabric, and get a ballpark price and bench-hours estimate — based on a working West.
- [Search the book](https://www.learntoupholster.com/search): Search every chapter of The Working Upholsterer's Bible — webbing, springing, stuffing, foam, buttoning, trimming, loose covers and more.
- [Springing (modern) — zigzag & no-sag springs](https://www.learntoupholster.com/springing-modern): Modern zigzag (sinuous / no-sag) springing: when to use it instead of coil, choosing the gauge, fitting the spring clips, the parallel-stripe layout.
- [Springing (traditional) — hand-tied coil springs](https://www.learntoupholster.com/springing-traditional): Traditional hand-tied coil-spring upholstery: selecting springs by height and gauge, placing the grid, bridling to the webbing, the eight-knot lashing.
- [Standards, regulations & bibliography — UK upholstery](https://www.learntoupholster.com/standards-regulations-and-bibliography): The administrative side of UK upholstery: the Furniture and Furnishings (Fire) (Safety) Regulations, an FR-materials checklist, the trade bodies worth.
- [Stool & Pouffe — Buttoned Drum Stool Project](https://www.learntoupholster.com/stool-pouffe): How to reupholster a stool or pouffe: the three types (square, drum, pouffe), a full buttoned drum-stool walk-through with the six-around-one pattern, the.
- [Stripping the Old Work — how to strip a chair for reupholstery](https://www.learntoupholster.com/stripping-the-old-work): How to strip an old chair for reupholstering: working outside-in, reading the tack holes in the old timber, what to save and what to bin, and the three-part.
- [Stuffing & stitched edges — the traditional sprung-edge seat](https://www.learntoupholster.com/stuffing-and-stitched-edges): The traditional stitched-edge first stuffing, stage by stage: bridle ties, first horsehair stuffing, packing and shaping the rim, the scrim cover, the blind.
- [Stuffover Dining Chair — A Step-Up Project](https://www.learntoupholster.com/stuffover-dining-chair): How to reupholster a traditional stuffover dining chair, step by step: stripping, webbing, the small stitched edge, calico, the top cover with the V-cut.
- [Terms of Use](https://www.learntoupholster.com/terms-of-use): The terms governing use of Learn to Upholster, including intellectual property, acceptable use, affiliate links and limitation of liability.
- [The Anatomy of an Upholstered Piece](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-anatomy-of-an-upholstered-piece): A shared vocabulary for every part of an upholstered chair: the outside panels of a wing-back, tub and ottoman; the traditional and modern seat in.
- [The Family Sofa from Heptonstall — a workshop story](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-family-sofa-from-heptonstall): A workshop story from Greenwood Upholstery: a 2019 Heal's sofa shredded by the family dogs but sound underneath — and why a good modern re-cover, saving a.
- [The Last Chair — the closing chapter](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-last-chair): The closing chapter of The Working Upholsterer's Bible: on signing your work, the notes upholsterers leave each other across centuries, what outlasts you.
- [The Pair of Nursing Chairs — a workshop story](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-pair-of-nursing-chairs): A workshop story from Greenwood Upholstery: restoring a pair of late-Victorian nursing chairs with 130 years of perfectly executed traditional upholstery.
- [The Toolkit — upholstery tools for beginners](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-toolkit): What tools do you need to start upholstering? The toolkit in three budget tiers — from a £120 beginner's kit to a working professional's bench — with what.
- [The Wing-Back That Wasn't a Howard — a workshop story](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-wing-back-that-wasnt-a-howard): A workshop story from Greenwood Upholstery: a wing-back armchair a London dealer called 'almost certainly a Howard' turns out to be a sound 1940s provincial.
- [The Workshop](https://www.learntoupholster.com/the-workshop): How to set up an upholstery workshop — the right space and doors, three-layer lighting, bench height that saves your back, dust and fire safety, fabric and.
- [Upholstery Tools & Calculators](https://www.learntoupholster.com/tools): Free upholstery calculators and tools: fabric yardage, reupholstery cost and time, and a deep-buttoning diamond calculator with marking diagrams.
- [Trimming & Finishing — gimp, welt, decorative nails & bottoming cloth](https://www.learntoupholster.com/trimming-and-finishing): The final-pass trims that finish a chair: gimp over the tack line, single and double welt (piping), decorative dome-headed nails at the right spacing, and.
- [Webbing — the load-bearing layer](https://www.learntoupholster.com/webbing): A complete guide to upholstery webbing: choosing 10-strand jute, the 8-strand interlace layout, the five-tack fixing pattern, tensioning with a strainer to.
- [Wing-Back Armchair — Full Traditional Restoration](https://www.learntoupholster.com/wing-back-armchair): The complete wing-back armchair restoration: anatomy, materials and hours, the six-stage build from strip to top cover, five stitched-edge pads, eight cover.

## Tools

Free calculators: fabric yardage, reupholstery cost & time, deep buttoning, foam & cushion spec, leather hides, and a UK fire-regulations checker with a printable compliance record. The search page includes an AI 'Ask the book' answer engine grounded solely in the book's text.
